framework: A path qualifies only if you can point at companies hiring for it or a role title that demonstrably exists - not a blog asserting it matters. Ranked by strength of that evidence, not by how often the skill is praised. Curated by hand; this file is editorial, which is why it is NOT inside <role>.json (that bundle is DOL-derived only).

paths.path: Ship your own interface in production code
paths.what_it_is: Design and implement the UI yourself, owning the live component system rather than handing a file to an engineer.
paths.why_it_signals: Linear, Vercel, Stripe and Anthropic all ship through design engineers, and the talent pool is explicitly not saturated. It removes your dependency on someone else to make your work real.

paths.what_it_is: A documented, versioned component set that other people build against, not a page of styles.
paths.why_it_signals: Design-systems-titled roles are posted independently of general product design, and titles like Digital Design Architect appear in the sponsor filings themselves.

paths.what_it_is: Design and test against WCAG properly - keyboard paths, screen readers, contrast, focus order - rather than treating it as a checklist at the end.
paths.why_it_signals: Accessibility Specialist is an established, separately-recruited title, and the European Accessibility Act and EN 301 549 turned compliance into a hiring driver rather than a preference. Note the honest limit: much of that hiring is compliance-led, so it is real demand but not always deep organisational buy-in.
